heading: Nonpay status for Congressional employees studying under Congressional staff fellowships
label: 4504 Nonpay status for Congressional employees studying under Congressional staff fellowships
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 130a of this title

Amendments1996— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), in par. (1) substituted “Chief Administrative Officer” for “Clerk”, in provisions following par. (2) substituted “the purposes of the provisions of law specified in subsection (b), if the award” for “the purposes of—Pub. L. 104–186“(A) subchapter III (relating to civil service retirement) of chapter 83 of title 5,“(B) chapter 87 (relating to Federal employees group life insurance) of title 5, and“(C) chapter 89 (relating to Federal employees group health insurance) of title 5,if the award”, “Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, as appropriate” for “Clerk of the House of Representatives, as appropriate”, and “Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives by records” for “Clerk of the House by records”, and added subsec. (b).
amendments: Amendments1996— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), in par. (1) substituted “Chief Administrative Officer” for “Clerk”, in provisions following par. (2) substituted “the purposes of the provisions of law specified in subsection (b), if the award” for “the purposes of—Pub. L. 104–186“(A) subchapter III (relating to civil service retirement) of chapter 83 of title 5,“(B) chapter 87 (relating to Federal employees group life insurance) of title 5, and“(C) chapter 89 (relating to Federal employees group health insurance) of title 5,if the award”, “Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, as appropriate” for “Clerk of the House of Representatives, as appropriate”, and “Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives by records” for “Clerk of the House by records”, and added subsec. (b).

children.text: whose compensation is disbursed by the Secretary of the Senate or the Chief Administrative Officer of the House of Representatives, and

who, on or after shall have been separated from employment with the Senate or House of Representatives in order to pursue certain studies under a congressional staff fellowship awarded by the American Political Science Association,January 1, 1963
subchapter III (relating to civil service retirement) of chapter 83 of title 5;
chapter 87 (relating to Federal employees group life insurance) of title 5; and
chapter 89 (relating to Federal employees group health insurance) of title 5.
